parties.name: Behoorlijk Bestuur voor Den Helder en Julianadorp
parties.fullName: Behoorlijk Bestuur voor Den Helder en Julianadorp

206751.explanation: For major projects affecting the municipality’s residents, there should be an advisory referendum whose results the municipality takes seriously. Participation is decision-making power.

206752.explanation: Regional cooperation is less effective than local. Local matters should be governed locally. Communication with other municipalities is, however, very important.

206753.explanation: The income from the tourist tax must also be spent on entrepreneurs.

206754.explanation: Keeping a pet should not be subject to additional taxes.

206756.explanation: Behoorlijk Bestuur supports more businesses, but not at the expense of the residents' quality of life.

206757.explanation: It is up to the farmers or entrepreneurs what they want to do with their agricultural land.

206758.explanation: Opening a campsite or overnight accommodations requires input from nearby residents. It must be assessed what benefits it brings to the residents of Den Helder and whether it causes no disturbance to neighbors.

206759.explanation: The residents of Den Helder and Julianadorp should not be unnecessarily burdened with costs. It is up to the residents to become more sustainable, with the municipality supporting them through subsidies. We are against a gas ban.

206760.explanation: Asylum seekers who are no longer in a procedure must be deported.

206761.explanation: All residents of Den Helder have the right to social rental housing. There is a shortage of social rental homes and we urge building more. Currently, more are being demolished than built. More suitable housing must be provided.

206762.explanation: We do not want regional accommodation for seasonal workers. Each region should take responsibility for the (temporary) housing of seasonal workers themselves.

206763.explanation: Behoorlijk Bestuur supports retaining fireplaces and wood stoves. We oppose imposition of climate rules from the EU.

206764.explanation: Behoorlijk Bestuur opposes solar meadows. This pollutes the natural environment and biodiversity.

206765.explanation: School swimming must return. This supports residents with financial limitations. It is an important investment in drowning safety and the future of children and the community as a whole.

206766.explanation: Money must be invested in school buildings where safety requires it. It is important that accessibility and the stay at school are safe.

206767.explanation: Music is healthy development for children. All residents should be able to apply for a municipal contribution.

206768.explanation: The municipality must invest in our monuments. There has been too little maintenance of our rich history of the line, monuments, and forts for too long. Our heritage is what attracts tourists to Den Helder. It is a source of income and makes Den Helder unique.

206769.explanation: Behoorlijk Bestuur supports restoring safety in residential neighborhoods. This is not necessarily about more enforcement officers, but about their presence-preferably 24 hours a day. As a helpful tool, drones could also be used to monitor multiple locations simultaneously.

206770.explanation: Behoorlijk Bestuur has always been against coffeeshops within city limits. They have a negative attracting effect on crime and thus nuisance.

206771.explanation: Behoorlijk Bestuur supports safety in neighborhoods. Cameras can contribute to safety but must not violate residents' privacy.

206773.explanation: Behoorlijk Bestuur supports starter loans but does not want to push young people to higher costs for large mortgages. We want to build more suitable housing for young people and seniors. Then a starter loan would not be necessary.

206774.explanation: Behoorlijk Bestuur believes that our own residents should be able to move from living at home to social rental housing and that the municipality must regain control of urgency. There is still a shortage of social rental homes.

206775.explanation: Behoorlijk Bestuur does not think that occupation should be part of the urgency criteria for obtaining social rental housing.

206776.explanation: Behoorlijk Bestuur has long advocated for more social housing.

206777.explanation: It is up to the initiator to decide whether to build homes or not.

206778.explanation: Due to the current housing shortage, it must be considered whether this can be allowed for a fixed period.

206779.explanation: Behoorlijk Bestuur observes that poverty is rising, so necessary assistance is even more essential.

206780.explanation: Behoorlijk Bestuur believes more investment must be made in employment. There are still too many labor companies abusing subsidies.

206781.explanation: Behoorlijk Bestuur supports the current system: exemption or reduction of (housing) costs for residents with financial difficulties.

206782.explanation: No experiments at the expense of residents.

206783.explanation: Behoorlijk Bestuur opposes cuts in youth education. More investment in youth training is necessary.

206784.explanation: Behoorlijk Bestuur thinks electric driving is allowed, but parking must be accessible to everyone.

206785.explanation: Mobility is not a luxury, even when public transport falls short.

206786.explanation: Behoorlijk Bestuur is not in favor of a 30 km/h speed limit throughout the municipality. Safe where needed and smooth where possible.

206787.explanation: Behoorlijk Bestuur supports improving accessibility to Den Helder. Den Helder deserves a good connection to the rest of the Netherlands.

206788.explanation: Behoorlijk Bestuur advocates keeping free parking. An accessible city is a vibrant city.

206789.explanation: Behoorlijk Bestuur supports a Small Modular Reactor (SMR). This new form of sustainable energy will bring great benefits to Den Helder.

206790.explanation: Behoorlijk Bestuur opposes the gas ban. It is the resident's choice. No ban, yes freedom of choice.

206791.explanation: Behoorlijk Bestuur says sustainability is allowed but must never be mandatory.

206792.explanation: The money for neighborhood teams has not been used efficiently. There is much uncertainty about the effects of the investments. There are currently too many neighborhood teams. Early help in the neighborhood works, provided the money goes to people, not paperwork.

206793.explanation: Care for animals is part of good local governance.

206794.explanation: Behoorlijk Bestuur is the creator of the Den Helder neighborhood prevention app. More visible police presence and enforcement officers are needed. Safety remains a government task.

206795.explanation: Behoorlijk Bestuur also holds the position of more suitable housing for seniors. Living independently longer with care nearby.

206796.explanation: Behoorlijk Bestuur supports retaining the presence of the neighborhood police officer. Visible neighborhood officers make neighborhoods safer.

206798.explanation: It is up to the entrepreneur to decide what to do with their land. Bulb cultivation is part of our living environment and culture. As the largest bulb-growing area in the region, Den Helder should support entrepreneurs, not restrict them.

206799.explanation: Behoorlijk Bestuur advocated improving accessibility for residents by establishing a municipality service office in Julianadorp. Home delivery means unnecessary costs for residents.

206800.explanation: Behoorlijk Bestuur supports binding referendums for large projects. Participation is already arranged for smaller-scale projects.

206801.explanation: Behoorlijk Bestuur believes the municipality should be able to use tourist tax flexibly and wisely.

206802.explanation: Legal obligation: yes, extra shelter: no.

206803.explanation: Behoorlijk Bestuur supports separate waste sorting. No waste tax per kilo, but practical and fair waste policy.

206805.explanation: Den Helder needs more enforcement and zero tolerance in strategic locations and areas where nuisance occurs.

206806.explanation: Behoorlijk Bestuur believes that rights must go hand in hand with duties.

206807.explanation: Behoorlijk Bestuur thinks green spaces should be preserved where appropriate.

206808.explanation: Behoorlijk Bestuur sees good shelter as necessary. Extra support is possible as long as it is focused on guidance, transition to housing and care, and not on maintaining problems.

206809.explanation: Language skills and participation from the start prevent disadvantages later on.

206810.explanation: Behoorlijk Bestuur opposes additional municipal investments in hydrogen. It is an expensive and uncertain technology that belongs to the national government and the market, not the municipal budget.

206811.explanation: Behoorlijk Bestuur is against a district heating network. Thorough research must first be conducted regarding costs, feasibility, and benefits for residents.

206813.explanation: Behoorlijk Bestuur believes care must be available when needed. The municipality may invest in extra capacity if it directly benefits residents and shortens waiting times - not in extra management or paperwork.

206814.explanation: Behoorlijk Bestuur believes those who need lifelong care deserve certainty and peace.

206815.explanation: Neighborhood initiatives strengthen involvement and livability, provided participation is voluntary and well supported.

206816.explanation: Behoorlijk Bestuur believes green areas should be protected with common sense.

206818.explanation: Behoorlijk Bestuur believes an accessible city center is a vibrant city.

206820.explanation: Behoorlijk Bestuur thinks public funds should go as much as possible to local entrepreneurs.

206821.explanation: Behoorlijk Bestuur finds neighborhood and facade gardens to be nice initiatives, but subsidies lead to arbitrariness and extra costs. The municipality should provide space and support where needed, not finance them structurally.

206751.explanation: Public participation is important, especially for newer topics.

206752.explanation: Our own municipality must always come first.

206753.explanation: Visitors should also contribute to our beautiful facilities.

206754.explanation: No unnecessary taxes for our residents.

206756.explanation: The municipality must facilitate, including the yes-if principle. Entrepreneurs should be given space.

206757.explanation: Agricultural land is entrepreneurial land in the broadest sense.

206758.explanation: Farm businesses must be given room for development.

206759.explanation: Working together towards a sustainable future.

206760.explanation: Den Helder has more than fulfilled its obligation.

206761.explanation: Our own residents should be given priority.

206762.explanation: Facilitate on own grounds where possible.

206763.explanation: Freedom must remain with the residents.

206764.explanation: Use rooftops first and cover parking spaces with solar panels instead of agricultural land.

206765.explanation: The safety of our children is paramount and can cost money.

206766.explanation: Education remains important, so does the building.

206769.explanation: Safety in neighborhoods and streets is paramount and can cost money.

206770.explanation: There are already enough of them.

206771.explanation: Safety is paramount and can cost money.

206775.explanation: We do not accept preferential treatment beyond the law for immigrants or recognized refugees regarding social rental housing in Den Helder. These rental homes should be made available to young starters, people who have been on the waiting list for a long time, teachers in education, police officers, nurses, and others who are indispensable to public services.

206776.explanation: A good balance is important. Den Helder already has many social rental homes.

206777.explanation: The municipality should facilitate, not co-finance.

206778.explanation: Having a roof over your head is more important than rules and regulations.

206783.explanation: Young people are the future, and the municipality therefore facilitates them.

206785.explanation: Our elderly deserve support where needed.

206789.explanation: Residents should benefit from cheap electricity.

206792.explanation: Working together with residents on assistance.

206793.explanation: Our animal friends deserve our support.

206795.explanation: Facilitating different forms of housing is important.

206798.explanation: Freedom for entrepreneurs is more important.

206800.explanation: Participation is important for our residents and entrepreneurs.

206802.explanation: Strong reduction of the current number.

206808.explanation: We are not a dumping ground.

206809.explanation: Backlogs for children are caught up this way.

206811.explanation: The choice lies with the residents themselves.

206813.explanation: Facilitating our own residents is the priority.

206815.explanation: Self-direction over the living environment of our residents and entrepreneurs.

206816.explanation: Trees are very important and must maintain their quality.

206818.explanation: Facilitating and competing with other municipalities is a priority.

206820.explanation: Our entrepreneurs have the local knowledge and skills and are socially involved.

206821.explanation: Self-direction over the living environment is important and often cheaper than municipal tenders.

206751.explanation: The VVD believes that the elected representatives (municipal council) should make the decisions. They are elected. VVD is positive about citizen participation and citizen panels.

206752.explanation: By working together, municipalities can share services such as IT, youth care, and waste processing. This reduces costs and prevents duplicate work. Together you are stronger in negotiations with the national government. It is not a goal in itself but a practical solution. Collaborate when possible, manage yourself when necessary.

206753.explanation: The VVD opposes tax increases unless the additional revenues are spent on matters relevant to tourism.

206754.explanation: The VVD opposes the introduction of extra taxes but supports facilitating off-leash areas and similar initiatives.

206755.explanation: The VVD supports projects that contribute to economic growth, promote employment, and make the city more attractive for visitors and entrepreneurs. This includes good promotion through city marketing.

206756.explanation: More space for businesses means more employment and strengthening of the local economy. Companies must be able to grow and be retained for the region.

206757.explanation: The VVD is not fundamentally against converting agricultural land into nature reserves, but is critical and cautious about it and will not actively promote it. It must not affect farmers' income models or create uncertainty for them.

206758.explanation: The municipality should not always allow it. It is not a vested right and the number is limited. It must concern ancillary activities that strengthen the farmers' income. We allow a maximum of 10 farm campsites in Den Helder.

206759.explanation: Structural support from the municipality is not automatic, but where we can offer a helping hand, the municipality is willing to encourage and support initiatives. This support is targeted and temporary.

206760.explanation: The VVD wants the municipality not to be structurally responsible for housing people without residence rights. Policy should be aimed at having persons who are not admitted leave the country as quickly as possible and return to their country of origin.

206761.explanation: The VVD believes that all residents on a waiting list have the right to housing. Automatic priority can lead to long waiting times and frustration among existing housing seekers.

206762.explanation: This fits within the employers' own responsibility and freedom to operate and must be done within clear rules and quality requirements.

206763.explanation: The use of open fireplaces and wood stoves must remain allowed, provided it is done safely and responsibly. Residents should be able to choose how to heat their homes themselves. So, do not prohibit, but manage nuisance by guidelines and encourage sustainable alternatives.

206764.explanation: We oppose a general ban. An agricultural entrepreneur may in principle decide for himself whether he wants to designate land for this purpose, provided a good balance is found between sustainable business operations and the maintenance of food production. Furthermore, it must not impact environmental quality.

206765.explanation: Promoting swimming and swimming skills is the responsibility of parents and/or caregivers, not schools. Any support in this area takes place through other channels, such as the sports fund and minimum income policy.

206766.explanation: We invest in new construction and renovation, such as for Schools by the Sea, so that students can learn in a modern, attractive, and safe environment.

206767.explanation: We view promoting musical education for children as the responsibility of parents and/or caregivers.

206768.explanation: Monuments must remain protected and receive priority where necessary.

206769.explanation: The VVD does advocate for "more police presence on the streets" and calls for attention to this in The Hague, but efforts must be practical and efficient.

206770.explanation: The current number of three coffeeshops meets the demand. The VVD is against expansion and automatically allowing extra coffeeshops beyond the current number.

206771.explanation: We will expand camera surveillance in unsafe spots in neighborhoods and districts.

206773.explanation: We are in favor of retaining the starter loan. It supports first-time homebuyers, encourages independence and self-reliance to have a home. The loan is not a gift but a loan that must be repaid.

206774.explanation: Preferably yes, to strengthen local livability and connections. Exceptions are possible, for example, when allocating to crucial professional groups in care, education, and safety.

206775.explanation: By exception, crucial professional groups may be given priority in allocation.

206776.explanation: VVD Den Helder does not favor adding more social rental homes to the housing stock. The minimum stock should be 30%. In Den Helder, this percentage is 38%. By building other types of homes, housing market flow is created.

206777.explanation: The VVD sees converting offices and other existing buildings into homes positively and as part of solving the housing shortage, but not solely funded by the municipality. We support a broader approach focusing on easing rules, procedures, and discouraging vacancies.

206778.explanation: Permanent residence in holiday parks must not hinder the development of the recreational and tourist sector. However, we do support making it potentially easier and less time-consuming to amend zoning plans for this purpose.

206779.explanation: The VVD emphasizes rewarding work. Poverty policy must provide basic security without discouraging efforts to find employment.

206780.explanation: The VVD believes that work is the norm and that work should always pay more than benefits. We create sheltered jobs with guidance and prospects for residents who are (not yet) ready for regular work.

206781.explanation: We believe everyone should contribute to the costs of society, preferably with as little taxation as possible. We are not in favor of waivers. Support to meet obligations could be provided through poverty policy.

206782.explanation: The VVD does not see a basic income as a realistic or desirable tool to combat poverty or guarantee basic security. Work must pay and financial support should be temporary and targeted so that people remain actively engaged in society and the labor market.

206783.explanation: Cutting costs is not a goal in itself. We want to reduce the number of children in care trajectories by at least 10% by implementing various measures such as a comprehensive network of social organizations around families with multiple problems and a clear care route map.

206784.explanation: The VVD wants electric driving to be possible and financially feasible for as many people as possible and focuses on expanding charging infrastructure.

206785.explanation: We consider mobility for the elderly and other target groups important and advocate an integrated offer of transportation for target groups, also looking at cooperation and connecting to initiatives in the region.

206786.explanation: The VVD opposes the idea that all roads within built-up areas must automatically have a 30 km/h speed limit. 30 km/h only where it is logical and necessary.

206787.explanation: We are strongly committed to the N77 variant to improve the accessibility of the northern part of North Holland.

206788.explanation: The VVD wants parking in the municipality of Den Helder to remain free of charge. This promotes tourism and business activity.

206789.explanation: We are positive about the realization of small modular reactors (SMRs) and see this as a serious and promising addition to our energy supply.

206790.explanation: The VVD opposes a general ban on gas connections in new residential areas. We support sustainability but not compulsory bans without sufficient freedom of choice, affordability, and technical feasibility. Residents and builders must retain freedom of choice in heating solutions.

206791.explanation: The VVD considers sustainability important, but residents should decide if, when, and how they make their homes more sustainable. Sustainability is encouraged, not forced.

206792.explanation: We invest in a comprehensive network of social organizations around families with multiple problems. This does not necessarily mean more money, but perhaps a different distribution and prioritization.

206793.explanation: The VVD only supports a wildlife shelter that is sustainable, affordable, and socially responsible.

206794.explanation: The VVD is positive about citizen initiatives that improve safety in neighborhoods. This always within clear frameworks and under the responsibility of the municipality.

206795.explanation: We want to realize alternative housing forms such as granny flats, home splits, senior cohousing, and clustered living arrangements with communal spaces.

206796.explanation: We focus on "more police presence on the streets" and good accessibility, approachability, and police presence in neighborhoods and districts.

206798.explanation: The VVD supports maintaining the economic activity of this sector. Agricultural entrepreneurs should have the freedom to run their businesses as long as they comply with environmental and safety regulations. We promote cleaner cultivation methods, reduced use of crop protection products, and water conservation.

206799.explanation: The VVD is positive about digitalization and improving services, including home delivery of passports and driving licenses, provided this can be done safely, reliably, and affordably.

206800.explanation: We find it important that residents participate and help make decisions that affect their neighborhood or district. For this, we already have neighborhood platforms and other participation and consultation opportunities.

206801.explanation: The VVD is critical of levying taxes unless the revenues are spent on matters that are also relevant to tourism.

206802.explanation: The VVD supports a balanced nationwide distribution of this obligation across all municipalities in the Netherlands and complies with the numbers applicable to Den Helder according to the distribution law.

206803.explanation: Paying for waste by the kilo is not always fair or practical. We do support stimulating waste separation through other efficient and targeted methods.

206805.explanation: VVD Den Helder advocates for a safe living environment. This does not necessarily require city marines. However, the VVD is open to investigating the idea and how it can be implemented within the organization's own framework.

206806.explanation: The VVD supports strict enforcement in this area and wants people on social assistance to return to work as soon as possible or otherwise actively participate in society.

206807.explanation: VVD Den Helder supports preserving the Green Lung with the caveat that future spatial planning considers the economy and housing development.

206808.explanation: VVD Den Helder favors financial support within the available resources. The provided aid must be effective and focused. The municipality takes its responsibility within the national frameworks.

206809.explanation: VVD Den Helder believes we must ensure that young children of newcomers regularly attend pre- and early childhood education.

206810.explanation: The VVD sees hydrogen as an important technology in the transition to more sustainable energy. We have a facilitating role in this.

206811.explanation: VVD Den Helder recognizes that sustainability and energy transition are necessary to meet climate goals but wants this to happen in a way that remains affordable for households and businesses.

206813.explanation: VVD Den Helder follows the Social Support Act (Wmo) procedure here. The emphasis is on the resident’s control, self-reliance, and appropriate assistance.

206814.explanation: The VVD believes people with a permanent disability should have access to the care and aids they need. This should be as simple as possible, close to home, and with personal control.

206815.explanation: VVD Den Helder provides space for citizen initiatives if they contribute to livability and do not impose unreasonable burdens. Additionally, participation must be voluntary and fit within clear agreements.

206816.explanation: Reforestation policy has already been established in Den Helder. This considers the value of felled trees and how they should be replaced-not necessarily one for one. VVD Den Helder supports sustainability policy provided it is balanced with livability and management.

206818.explanation: VVD Den Helder favors an attractive city center. By offering free parking, we keep the city center appealing to visitors, residents, and entrepreneurs.

206820.explanation: VVD Den Helder emphasizes that entrepreneurs and SMEs are important for the local economy. The procurement process must be transparent according to the law and policy rules and provide equal opportunities. Access for local entrepreneurs should be maximized within these rules.

206821.explanation: VVD Den Helder has agreed to co-finance the installation of facade gardens.

206751.explanation: The CDA wants residents to be involved early in (large) projects. An advisory referendum can be a means to this end but costs approximately €150,000 and is costly to organize. A binding referendum is not permitted.

206752.explanation: Den Helder does not stand alone. Regional cooperation strengthens our position towards the province and national government and helps retain facilities such as the hospital. This also yields much in the social domain. The Coastal Bus is a good example of CDA cooperation.

206753.explanation: In recent years, the tourist tax has been significantly increased as a budget balancing measure. For the CDA, it is important that we do not price ourselves out of the market compared to other municipalities in the region.

206754.explanation: Past experience shows that the dog tax yields little and requires extra enforcement and administration.

206755.explanation: City marketing has played an important role in recent years to put Den Helder on the map. We as residents have also benefited from this, think of the Helder Light Festival.

206756.explanation: There is a large demand for space for business parks including the port. Especially now, we must ensure that space is used smartly and efficiently, including looking at expanding the seaport.

206757.explanation: We assess converting agricultural land on a case-by-case basis and based on careful consideration. Julianadorp is part of the largest contiguous bulb flower area in the world. We are proud of this and take it as a starting point in our decisions.

206758.explanation: We support small-scale tourism at farms. This enriches the tourist offer. However, it must be carefully managed and compatible with the environment.

206759.explanation: We welcome it when residents jointly generate or store sustainable energy, for example in a neighborhood battery. The municipality already offers various energy subsidies but remains alert to new developments.

206760.explanation: Den Helder cannot arrange shelter for people who are not allowed to stay here. That is the responsibility of the national government. Within legal frameworks, we do consider a humane approach important.

206761.explanation: Municipalities have a legal task to house permit holders. This requires careful choices, keeping in mind other housing seekers and focusing on more and faster housing construction.

206762.explanation: Seasonal workers should be housed as much as possible on the employer’s own premises. Additional shelter for employees from other agricultural companies is possible as long as the main function remains intact. This prevents regular housing from being used for this purpose.

206763.explanation: We are not in favor of a general ban on wood burning. However, we consider it important to limit nuisance and health risks and that people take their surroundings into account.

206764.explanation: When placing solar panels, preserving the landscape and the agricultural function is our priority. We prefer to place solar panels on roofs and business parks, with careful landscaping integration.

206765.explanation: The CDA chooses for School Swimming 2.0: every child receives swimming lessons at their own pace during primary education. For less affluent families, the municipality pays via the Youth Sports Fund. A general subsidy for traditional school swimming is too burdensome for schools.

206766.explanation: Agreed. This is good for the learning achievements of youth.

206767.explanation: Yes, we also see great examples in practice of music education at schools provided by Triade.

206768.explanation: Heritage is valuable for the identity and character of Den Helder and requires ongoing attention and care. Multi-year investments are planned for strengthening the Lines in the coming period. This is urgently needed.

206769.explanation: Extra enforcement officers (BOAs) can be added if necessary, feasible, and affordable. 24-hour coverage is not an option because they are not police.

206770.explanation: No, three coffeeshops are sufficient. Den Helder should invest in education about substance use and prevention of addictions.

206771.explanation: We need to increase the sense of security in Den Helder through a mix of measures. The use of (mobile) cameras is one of these, but it is not a goal in itself and is a consideration for the mayor.

206773.explanation: The starter loan has been an initiative from the CDA. In recent times, the budget ceiling has often been raised due to its success. We are therefore in favor of it.

206774.explanation: Priority for home seekers from the local municipality helps young people to continue living in Den Helder and promotes movement within the housing market, which aligns with our focus on youth housing.

206775.explanation: Due to high housing prices, it is difficult for people in vital professions to live here. Targeted priority helps retain teachers, healthcare workers, and police officers in Den Helder.

206776.explanation: New construction must connect to the existing housing stock. Den Helder already has a lot of social housing. We consider sticking to a fixed percentage unwise. We aim for a healthy mix, now with extra attention to housing for young people and the elderly.

206777.explanation: We support converting vacant offices into homes. If financial support is needed for this, for example by reducing fees, that is open for discussion.

206778.explanation: Holiday homes are primarily intended for vacations. Den Helder currently has a healthy tourist offer. Permanent residence changes the economic structure of tourism. A holiday home can temporarily offer a solution for parents, for example, who are in the midst of a divorce.

206779.explanation: We are not in favor of this. However, we do believe the municipality should provide more targeted help to those who need it, for example by better identifying low-income groups.

206780.explanation: Subsidized jobs are not a goal in themselves. They can be useful if they contribute to activation and participation, such as at Probedrijven, the Hortus, and Landschap Noord-Holland.

206781.explanation: As long as there are residents with limited financial means, remission of debts remains an important tool to prevent financial problems.

206782.explanation: We are not in favor of a basic income.

206783.explanation: We oppose cuts to youth work and have previously preserved school social work because prevention and guidance of young people is very important.

206784.explanation: Due to the shortage of charging points, expansion is needed to encourage electric driving.

206785.explanation: From neighboring municipalities, we hear positive reports about the demand-responsive bus service. It would be great if that network could be expanded. With a demand-responsive bus, we support elderly and vulnerable residents in their accessibility.

206786.explanation: A general 30 km/h speed limit hinders traffic flow. We prefer tailored solutions, with lower speeds in residential areas and busy residential streets.

206787.explanation: The construction of the N77 is necessary for better accessibility of Den Helder.

206788.explanation: We support parking with the blue disc in certain zones where parking pressure is high. This can be further expanded to, for example, Willemsoord and the area around the hospital.

206789.explanation: The CDA supports an SMR (Small Modular Reactor). We believe this process should be conducted carefully. We must also realize that the choice of location is primarily a national issue where one can question whether Den Helder is the most suitable location for this facility.

206790.explanation: New build homes should in principle be constructed without a gas connection to promote sustainability. However, we must monitor affordability and feasibility, so exceptions should remain possible. Also, to avoid delaying construction due to additional regulatory burdens.

206791.explanation: The municipality has many subsidy options available for making homes more sustainable. We see sustainability as a personal choice and responsibility of the owner. However, we do believe the municipality should actively encourage this, for example through subsidies.

206792.explanation: In Den Helder, there is a lot of family-related issues, often resulting in (contentious) divorces. This should be prevented as much as possible. Neighborhood teams play an important role in this.

206793.explanation: We support the construction of a new wildlife shelter. This does not necessarily have to be at the Helderse Vallei. We do believe the costs should be proportionate to the service provided. The last plan cost more than one and a half million due to nature compensation.

206794.explanation: We believe the government should take responsibility here, not individual groups.

206795.explanation: We focus on a healthy mix of housing, with appropriate housing forms for the elderly in cooperation with care providers and housing corporations.

206796.explanation: The deployment of neighborhood police officers requires tailored solutions. Per neighborhood, what is needed is examined so that police capacity is effectively used based on consideration by the mayor and police.

206798.explanation: We must strengthen the character of bulb cultivation, also in the interest of tourism.

206799.explanation: Yes, we have raised this before. The neighboring municipalities also do this. However, it must remain possible to pick up your passport at city hall.

206800.explanation: The municipal council is a reflection of society. The municipality already involves residents through 'In Gesprek Den Helder' and participation groups. There are also advisory platforms such as the neighborhood platform, the Social Domain Advisory Council, and the youth advisory council which was restarted on our initiative.

206801.explanation: A portion of the proceeds must concretely flow back to investments important for the recreational sector.

206802.explanation: Den Helder has accommodated more asylum seekers than the region for years, and we are proud of this. Now other municipalities are taking their turn, and we follow the distribution law. In emergencies due to hunger or violence, we remain willing to provide temporary extra shelter.

206803.explanation: We first choose to improve waste collection and have made concrete proposals for this. Paying per kilo of waste (diftar) is not appropriate in our view.

206805.explanation: The municipality should not enter neighborhoods with too many different points of contact.

206806.explanation: Rights and obligations belong together. By stricter monitoring of the job application obligation, we prevent abuse of social assistance.

206807.explanation: Thanks to the CDA, the Green Lung remains intact.

206808.explanation: Good reception is necessary, but more financial support is not automatic. First, organize more efficiently within the current budget.

206809.explanation: A strong start in early childhood determines the later school career. Early childhood education (VVE) programs strengthen language, social skills, and self-confidence. Extra investment helps to catch up on delays early, increases equal opportunities, and delivers long-term social benefits.

206810.explanation: In the discussion about SMRs and other sustainable energy sources, we choose a future-proof energy mix in which hydrogen can play a role. The technology is still developing, but by offering space now, hydrogen can give an impulse to the port without extra municipal funds.

206811.explanation: We monitor projects closely. Success is not guaranteed, which is why we value an open and transparent process. The CDA stands for shared responsibility and understands that we cannot please everyone. The plan in De Schooten is currently not proceeding due to financial reasons.

206813.explanation: Extra personnel help in the short term, but the organization of care must improve structurally. At the beginning of the council term in 2022, we had concrete goals included in the budget through a unanimously adopted amendment. Steps have been taken, but unfortunately we are not there yet.

206814.explanation: We have asked the alderman questions about this. In practice, this already partly happens, but it remains important to keep in dialogue with those involved to see if their aids are still appropriate. New technologies may now offer better solutions.

206815.explanation: Yes, this helps foster community spirit in the neighborhood. The municipality remains ultimately responsible.

206816.explanation: We believe the municipality should give every new resident a tree. The goal is to increase the absolute number of trees in our municipality and expand public green spaces.

206818.explanation: We support parking with the blue disc in certain zones where parking pressure is high. This can be further expanded to, for example, Willemsoord and the area around the hospital.

206820.explanation: Yes, it remains important that, despite stringent procurement standards, local entrepreneurs have a chance to obtain contracts from the municipality.

206821.explanation: Subsidies for community gardens contribute to togetherness. A facade garden is a small planting the size of one pavement tile in front of your own home. Very charming, but the costs to provide such small-scale subsidies would exceed the cost of the planting many times over.

206751.explanation: D66 supports a corrective binding referendum, provided it is carefully designed and respects fundamental rights and minorities. This allows direct citizen participation. The cabinet has taken initial steps in recent years to enable this type of referendum. This increases transparency and involvement. Citizens' assemblies are also a valuable addition to strengthen democracy.

206752.explanation: Regional cooperation is essential to improve quality and efficiency. At the same time, it is important that Den Helder retains its own identity and decision-making authority.

206753.explanation: Tourist tax is already at the level of surrounding municipalities. Further increases could harm tourism and local businesses.

206754.explanation: Dog owners have their own responsibility for public spaces. Additional taxes are unnecessary and could create barriers to well-being and companionship.

206755.explanation: Den Helder offers space, quality of life, and talent. Targeted city marketing strengthens economic opportunities, especially with the growth of the navy and port.

206756.explanation: A strong business climate is important. Expansion is possible, provided it is sustainable and carefully integrated. We see sufficient space for expansion at Kooypunt.

206757.explanation: D66 seeks balance between agriculture and nature. Flower bulb growers can play an important role in sustainability and deserve clarity and perspective.

206758.explanation: This strengthens entrepreneurship and the local economy, provided it fits the environment and does not harm nature and livability.

206759.explanation: D66 is open to innovative and sustainable solutions. Generating sustainable energy together increases support, lowers costs, and speeds up the energy transition.

206760.explanation: Shelter without prospects is neither humane nor effective. Focus on guidance toward return offers clarity and dignity.

206761.explanation: A home is the foundation for integration, work, and participation in society.

206762.explanation: Employers are responsible for decent housing and should not shift that burden onto the housing market.

206763.explanation: Wood burning can be harmful to health and air quality. D66 therefore wants to strongly discourage its use through education, burn alerts, and clear rules. A general ban goes too far.

206764.explanation: Sustainable energy requires tailored approaches; a general ban is unwise.

206765.explanation: School swimming increases safety, health, and equal opportunities for children. D66 supports a swimming diploma for every child, which could be arranged through schools, but other options also exist.

206766.explanation: Fresh and safe schools are essential for learning and well-being.

206767.explanation: D66 supports a rich school day, with music lessons as part of that. Cultural education should be accessible to every child, regardless of income.

206768.explanation: Monuments tell the story of Den Helder and deserve protection.

206769.explanation: Safety requires smart deployment and cooperation, not automatically more enforcement officers.

206770.explanation: Soft drug policy requires careful regulation, not unchecked growth.

206771.explanation: For D66, prevention is paramount. Targeted camera surveillance can help, but only if it is truly focused and respects privacy.

206773.explanation: Much use has been made of this in recent years. The starter loan helps young people take their first step on the housing market.

206774.explanation: Connection with the municipality may be taken into account within fair and transparent rules.

206775.explanation: Crucial professions are vital for the municipality; sometimes it is necessary to give certain groups priority. However, at least 50% must always remain available for regular home seekers.

206776.explanation: D66 stands for mixed neighborhoods. About 30% social housing in a neighborhood contributes to balance and livability.

206777.explanation: Building more is not the only solution; effective use of existing buildings is also important. A good example is the former V&D building, where shops will be on the ground floor and apartments above.

206778.explanation: Holiday homes are not a structural solution to the housing shortage.

206779.explanation: By slightly raising the threshold, we prevent people from falling between the cracks.

206780.explanation: Work is the goal; guidance toward regular employment is therefore preferred.

206781.explanation: Waiving charges prevents debt and increases security of existence. We must prevent a small-scale repeat of the benefits scandal.

206782.explanation: Previous basic income experiments offer valuable lessons. D66 wants to utilize these and advocates simplification at the national level.

206783.explanation: Youth work prevents problems and strengthens the social foundation.

206784.explanation: Charging points make clean transport accessible to everyone, but this must align with actual demand. Recently, the council drafted a good plan that will be implemented in the coming years.

206785.explanation: A call bus can be part of a broader approach to accessible transport. Independence and accessibility for vulnerable groups are important.

206786.explanation: This increases traffic safety and livability. Custom solutions remain necessary on through roads.

206787.explanation: This can help improve accessibility, which is important for economic development and thus the livability of the municipality.

206788.explanation: Free parking does not exist; only the costs are not passed on to the user. To ensure a lively (city) center, it is important not to introduce paid parking now.

206789.explanation: New energy forms such as small nuclear reactors deserve an open mind, especially when companies want to invest in them. A nuclear reactor can contribute to a sustainable energy mix.

206790.explanation: New construction must be future-proof and gas-free. This also contributes to the affordability of energy bills.

206791.explanation: Freedom is important, but collective solutions can be affordable and effective, especially when done at the neighborhood level.

206792.explanation: Strong neighborhood teams provide quick, appropriate help close to home, but can also work preventively and thus save money in the long term.

206793.explanation: D66 has long advocated for this, and this plan must finally be realized. A good wildlife shelter contributes to animal welfare and can also play an educational role.

206794.explanation: Safety is a task for professionals, not for citizen patrols.

206795.explanation: Suitable housing forms help older people live independently and stay connected, which can, for example, counteract loneliness.

206796.explanation: The police determine their own deployment. D66 sees neighborhood support points as a good way to connect with residents who need that.

206798.explanation: D66 chooses sustainability through clear rules, supervision, and cooperation, not through generic bans.

206799.explanation: This is already possible, and we want to keep it that way. Better service makes the government more accessible to everyone.

206800.explanation: In the past period, we have already arranged a citizens' assembly or Taskforce for sustainability; we see this as a good way to involve residents in important themes and thus strengthen democracy.

206801.explanation: Tourism also uses general facilities, so it would be strange to limit the use of its revenues. Efforts should be able to be broader.

206802.explanation: D66 welcomes the dispersion law that ensures an equal distribution of asylum seeker reception. It is also useful to use existing facilities for proper reception, against the right compensation.

206803.explanation: Paying for waste by the kilo can work, based on the principle 'the polluter pays'. However, this must be feasible and effective, for example, without causing more litter.

206805.explanation: D66 supports preventive policy, not repressive policy. In neighborhoods perceived as unsafe, the police can take appropriate measures.

206806.explanation: Extra checks do not contribute to finding work more quickly. Trust and guidance do.

206807.explanation: Protecting nature is investing in health and quality of life.

206808.explanation: The current shelter suffices; the focus must be on guidance and prevention.

206809.explanation: Early childhood education (VVE) gives children a fair start and prevents disadvantages. However, it must be of good quality; in recent periods, we have repeatedly called attention to this. We will remain vigilant about the quality and effectiveness of VVE.

206810.explanation: This can help make the port or other industries more sustainable, especially in times of grid congestion.

206811.explanation: The energy transition must remain affordable for residents.

206813.explanation: In recent years, great work has been done to speed up Social Support Act (Wmo) applications. As a result, there are no backlogs anymore. For ongoing care, the Long-term Care Act (Wlz) is the appropriate framework. The municipality carefully guides residents in this.

206814.explanation: People with disabilities deserve certainty and clarity. It is important, therefore, to regularly check whether the care is still appropriate.

206815.explanation: Resident initiatives strengthen involvement and ownership; it should not lead to cluttering the public space.

206816.explanation: Green compensation keeps the city livable and climate-resilient. Who wouldn't want to cool off in a park on a hot summer day?

206818.explanation: Nothing is free; however, we are dealing with a city center under development. Introducing paid parking now is not good for a lively city center.

206820.explanation: We are proud of our local entrepreneurs; where possible, they should be given preference in tenders. This is good for employment and saves unnecessary travel, thereby strengthening the local economy.

206821.explanation: D66 will always support good initiatives that contribute to a pleasant living environment. In doing so, we mainly try to focus on what is possible instead of falling into rules and procedures.

206751.explanation: GroenLinks wants residents to have a say in plans. Sometimes a plan has such an impact on residents that a referendum is justified. Ideally, a referendum is not needed because residents already have early input in policy.

206752.explanation: Sometimes cooperation with other municipalities is necessary. For example, to get important matters from the national government done, such as accessibility. Smart cooperation can also make things cheaper and fairer, which benefits residents. Local policies must remain possible.

206753.explanation: GroenLinks focuses on livability and affordable facilities for residents. When budgets are tight, tourist tax can be raised, but otherwise, this is unnecessary.

206754.explanation: Charging dog tax does not improve facilities for dogs; the money goes to general funds. Pets are important to people, and GroenLinks does not want to create a financial barrier to owning a dog.

206755.explanation: Attracting companies is important for the economy. The type of companies must be considered; for example, we do not want polluting companies.

206756.explanation: GroenLinks wants sustainable use of space. New and larger industrial estates will only be created if this does not harm nature and livability.

206757.explanation: GroenLinks wants more nature and greenery in and around the city. Agricultural land can be used for nature or housing.

206758.explanation: The municipality must assess per location whether ancillary activities fit with nature and the environment. Not everything is automatically allowed.

206759.explanation: Residents should be able to generate energy together. The municipality can support this financially or practically.

206760.explanation: GroenLinks believes that people in need must receive shelter. Everyone who needs help gets a place, even if they are not allowed to stay in the Netherlands. As long as people are within our municipality, we must care for them.

206761.explanation: Status holders have the same rights as other residents. They must be able to participate quickly in society. This works better from a home than from a reception center.

206762.explanation: Seasonal workers must have good housing. This does not necessarily have to be on the company site where they work, which also reduces dependence on the employer.

206763.explanation: Traditional wood burning is bad for health and air quality. GroenLinks wants a clean city.

206764.explanation: Sustainable energy is necessary. GroenLinks does not want to simply ban it. However, priority should first be given to solar panels on roofs.

206765.explanation: Sports and water safety for children are important. The municipality should support and encourage this. Perhaps not with swimming lessons during school hours, as this costs schools a lot of teaching time, but we must ensure every child learns to swim.

206766.explanation: Healthy, fresh, and sustainable school buildings are important for children. The municipality must ensure that all school buildings owned by the municipality are well maintained and meet modern standards.

206767.explanation: Culture and music should be accessible to all children. It supports their development and enjoyment. Every school should have a music teacher giving lessons.

206768.explanation: Heritage is part of Den Helder and must be preserved. Budget cuts threaten historical buildings.

206769.explanation: GroenLinks emphasizes prevention and proximity. Extra 24/7 enforcement is not the solution. Community officers (Boa's) must not be deployed instead of the police.

206770.explanation: Coffeeshops must be well distributed throughout the city.

206771.explanation: Social safety is important. You don’t achieve that with cameras alone. Cameras do not provide real safety. GroenLinks prefers more neighborhood police officers and youth work.

206773.explanation: Starters should be able to get help buying their first home. This helps mobility out of rental housing. The starter’s loan can drive up prices. Therefore, we want to explore other ways to help people purchase their first house.

206774.explanation: Housing construction is primarily for people from the region, so local residents find a home faster. This happens through the housing corporations' points system.

206775.explanation: Teachers, healthcare workers, and police are important for the city. They must be able to quickly find housing.

206776.explanation: We want a large portion of homes to remain affordable because many of our residents have lower incomes. The housing shortage lies in the social rental sector and the mid-priced rental homes. Therefore, GroenLinks wants 40% to be built in each of these sectors and 20% in the expensive segment.

206777.explanation: Vacant offices can become homes. This partially solves the housing shortage and uses space smartly.

206778.explanation: Temporary use is possible to quickly give people shelter. For permanent living, this is not desirable.

206779.explanation: More people should be able to get help with financial problems. GroenLinks wants to reduce financial stress.

206780.explanation: Some people need extra support to find work. This helps social equality. The number of subsidized jobs should match the demand.

206781.explanation: GroenLinks wants more people with low incomes to receive tax assistance.

206782.explanation: GroenLinks supports experiments with a basic income. This helps people financially and simplifies rules.

206783.explanation: Youth work prevents problems and crime. It should be strengthened. The number of youth with problems is increasing, and youth work helps avoid higher costs.

206784.explanation: Electric driving helps make the city cleaner. GroenLinks wants more charging points.

206785.explanation: Transport must also work for elderly and people who have difficulty using public transportation. It should complement taxi vouchers.

206786.explanation: Slower driving makes streets safer and more pleasant for children and residents.

206787.explanation: GroenLinks chooses public transport, cycling, and walking. New highways do not fit this vision. They heavily damage the landscape and offer little improvement in accessibility.

206788.explanation: Paid parking remains an option. GroenLinks wants smart parking standards.

206789.explanation: Nuclear energy is dangerous and not sustainable. GroenLinks chooses solar, wind, and hydrogen.

206790.explanation: Since 2018 it has been nationally determined that homes no longer need to be connected to gas. The municipality must follow this and make no exceptions. New homes must be sustainable and future-proof.

206791.explanation: The municipality works with residents but also provides direction for the energy transition. Requirements are imposed by the national government.

206792.explanation: Help close to home prevents problems with youth and families. The neighborhood teams are easily accessible, so help can be provided quickly.

206793.explanation: The wildlife shelter is a very long-running project that must now be completed quickly.

206794.explanation: Safety is a task for professionals. Citizens must not be solely responsible. GroenLinks opposes all forms of citizen patrols, as they can lead to vigilante justice and abuse.

206795.explanation: Elderly people should have suitable housing options between independent living and nursing homes. When elderly move to appropriate housing, it helps free up family homes, thus unlocking the housing market.

206796.explanation: Neighborhood police officers create trust and social safety in the community.

206798.explanation: Bulb cultivation still uses chemical pesticides. Less bulb cultivation means less poison and is better for nature, health, and the living environment. We want organic bulb cultivation.

206799.explanation: Services must be easy, and delivery methods should be arranged according to needs for people who cannot come to the town hall.

206800.explanation: Residents must genuinely be able to think along about important decisions. A citizens' assembly can help with this.

206801.explanation: It is a general tax. Tourism affects many residents and aspects of the municipality. Therefore, tourist tax money must be usable broadly.

206802.explanation: Solidarity goes beyond the minimum. People in need receive help. We have been doing this very well in Den Helder for a long time, and there is no reason to stop.

206803.explanation: Waste separation is important. It seems fair to charge people who produce a lot of waste more. But a lot of waste is already being dumped illegally; charging per kilo risks even more illegal dumping by residents.

206805.explanation: Safety is managed with neighborhood police officers, public order officers, and youth work, not with city marines.

206806.explanation: People should receive more help and guidance, not extra strict control.

206807.explanation: Green spaces and parks must be protected. No construction is allowed here.

206808.explanation: Everyone has the right to shelter and a safe place to sleep.

206809.explanation: Children of newcomers must get a good start in education. The younger and earlier children receive education, the faster they learn Dutch. In this way, they also help their family integrate.

206810.explanation: Hydrogen can help to store and transport sustainably generated energy from wind power.

206811.explanation: People must not be forced to pay more money due to sustainability measures in the form of a heat network.

206813.explanation: People must be able to quickly apply for help and facilities. They should not have to wait for care and/or assistance.

206814.explanation: People with disabilities need certainty and ongoing support. It is only costly and inconvenient to issue short-term indications when it is clear that the need for help will not disappear.

206815.explanation: Residents may participate in their own neighborhood and green projects.

206816.explanation: Trees are important for clean air, water management, and cooling in the city.

206818.explanation: GroenLinks keeps the option of paid parking open. Car use is not leading.

206820.explanation: Local companies get a fair chance in contracts and tenders.

206821.explanation: Green in the city is healthy. The municipality must support residents financially, but above all practically.

206751.explanation: PVV Den Helder supports all possibilities for citizen participation.

206753.explanation: PVV Den Helder believes that our municipality should be financially on the same level as neighboring municipalities. PVV Den Helder believes our municipality has more to offer in quality.

206755.explanation: PVV Den Helder sees this as a means to attract more companies and jobs to our municipality.

206756.explanation: PVV Den Helder believes this creates more employment and strengthens our municipality economically. The municipality of Den Helder has enough capacity to realize this.

206759.explanation: PVV Den Helder believes everyone is free to pursue sustainability and has their own responsibility in this.

206760.explanation: PVV Den Helder wants 0% asylum seeker shelter.

206761.explanation: PVV Den Helder believes priority for social rental housing should be for our own residents.

206768.explanation: PVV Den Helder wants full restoration of our fortifications and that heritage is actively used as an economic engine.

206769.explanation: PVV Den Helder believes that due to continuing unsafe situations and shortages in the National Police, extra public order officers can increase safety.

206771.explanation: PVV Den Helder believes that due to increasing unsafe situations and shortages in the National Police and municipal enforcement officers, more camera surveillance/ANPR must be deployed.

206773.explanation: PVV Den Helder considers this absolutely necessary so that young people from our municipality get the chance to buy their first home.

206784.explanation: PVV Den Helder believes more parking spaces must be created for all types of vehicles.

206786.explanation: PVV Den Helder is a strong supporter of traffic safety but opposes false security. 30 km/h zones do not necessarily contribute to traffic safety and smooth traffic flow.

206787.explanation: PVV Den Helder believes that substantial improvement in accessibility must come as soon as possible.

206789.explanation: PVV Den Helder considers a Small Modular Reactor (SMR) a reliable, affordable, and safe energy source that is future-proof and creates high-quality jobs.

206790.explanation: PVV Den Helder is against any form of forced sustainability.

206793.explanation: PVV Den Helder believes that after years of postponement, the decision must now be made.

206794.explanation: PVV Den Helder is the only party to place high value on safety. We support any form of citizen participation that can increase safety.

206796.explanation: PVV Den Helder believes safety must always be visible.

206799.explanation: PVV Den Helder believes official and privacy-sensitive documents must be collected to prevent identity fraud.

206800.explanation: PVV Den Helder supports every form of citizen participation.

206801.explanation: PVV Den Helder believes that all municipal income must be spent responsibly.

206802.explanation: PVV Den Helder believes all asylum seeker centers must be closed immediately and does not want new asylum centers to be allowed.

206805.explanation: PVV Den Helder believes these specially appointed professionals must fully focus on persistent safety problems. These city marines have decisional power and can cut through various layers of government and institutions.

206808.explanation: PVV Den Helder believes our municipality must not attract homeless people from other municipalities. More can be achieved with the same budget.

206809.explanation: PVV Den Helder believes all children from our municipality must be treated equally.

206811.explanation: PVV Den Helder is against any form of forced sustainability measures.

206813.explanation: PVV Den Helder believes that more can be achieved by working more efficiently.

206820.explanation: PVV Den Helder believes that local entrepreneurs should be invited first for tenders.

206751.explanation: Residents elect a municipal council every four years to make decisions. Organizing a referendum is difficult and very expensive. We prefer to invest that money in Den Helder, Julianadorp, and Huisduinen.

206752.explanation: The municipality of Den Helder is a central municipality. We cooperate with municipalities in the region only if it is legally required or if it clearly benefits the residents of Den Helder, Julianadorp, and Huisduinen.

206753.explanation: Tourists use the facilities in Den Helder, Julianadorp, and Huisduinen. It is fair that they contribute financially. This way we keep our municipality attractive for tourists and livable for our residents.

206754.explanation: A dog brings companionship, exercise, and safety. We do not want to punish that with an extra tax. The cleaning of dog waste must be paid from general funds.

206755.explanation: The image of Den Helder has improved enormously in recent years. This is also due to good advertising (city marketing). It attracts tourists, residents, and businesses. That is good for employment and the economy of our city.

206756.explanation: Entrepreneurs must have the space to grow because that creates jobs. Expansion is allowed only if it fits in the space and does not cause nuisance to the surroundings. These business parks should not be used, for example, for shops.

206757.explanation: Our bulb fields and farms are important for our history and economy. Den Helder, Julianadorp, and Huisduinen already have a lot of beautiful nature. We want to be careful with the fertile soil that our farmers and growers so urgently need.

206758.explanation: We encourage entrepreneurship, but we also want to counteract 'clutter' of the landscape. Campsites cannot be placed just anywhere. It must fit in the environment. Farmers who want this should receive advice on what requirements must be met.

206759.explanation: We think green energy and citizen initiatives are very good, but the municipality is not a bank. There are already enough national subsidies. The municipality should mainly help by keeping rules simple, not by giving money directly.

206760.explanation: We are happy to provide shelter to people fleeing war who have a future in the Netherlands. But people who are not allowed to stay here must return. Unfortunately, we see that this group often causes nuisance, and shelter offers false hope for this group.

206761.explanation: The housing shortage is high for everyone: youth, elderly, and status holders. Always giving priority to one group feels unfair to many people. We want a fair distribution based on waiting time. The real solution is: building more for all target groups.

206762.explanation: We see more in cooperation between companies. Not every farmer needs employees in the same season. We prefer good, shared locations with supervision rather than scattered housing on every farmyard.

206763.explanation: We are against a total ban. However, we find education important to prevent nuisance. With modern stoves and common sense, heating is fine. We will not prescribe how people heat their homes.

206764.explanation: This should be the owner's choice. However, we require that the surroundings must not suffer from it. We preferably see solutions where the land is used doubly (for example, agriculture and energy generation).

206765.explanation: Swimming lessons during school time reduce lesson time for other subjects. For parents who cannot afford swimming lessons, there is already the Youth Sports Fund. This way every child can still get a diploma.

206766.explanation: Where necessary, the municipality must work with schools on buildings where students can play and learn safely and healthily.

206767.explanation: There are already subsidies for culture, such as the program 'The Rich School Day.' We prefer to use our money targeted for children who really need it.

206768.explanation: Our heritage is the gold of Den Helder. Think of the Defense Line of Den Helder. We must cherish the past and preserve it for the future.

206769.explanation: Yes, we are in favor of more municipal enforcement officers. Extra public order officers increase visibility and safety. Vacancies are not always easy to fill. The police remain primarily responsible, but we want to invest together with the police in enforcement, also outside office hours.

206770.explanation: There are already enough coffeeshops in Den Helder to meet demand. We do not think it is necessary to have more.

206771.explanation: Safety comes first. Cameras assist the police, but we only install them in problem areas and the entertainment district. Not just anywhere in the city.

206773.explanation: It is difficult for youth and first-time buyers to buy a house. We do not want them to have to leave our municipality. The starter loan helps them to buy that first home.

206774.explanation: People who already live, work, or have family here often lose out. Therefore, we find it fair to give our own residents priority in the allocation of social rental housing.

206775.explanation: There is a great shortage of staff in important sectors. As far as we are concerned, this may also apply to other professions that clearly add value to the Den Helder community. Without housing, these people will not come.

206776.explanation: Social rental housing is needed, but a strict requirement of 40% often makes building too expensive for developers. Then nothing gets built in the end. We would rather have affordable owner-occupied homes and mid-range rental for better flow.

206777.explanation: Converting offices into (student) housing is a fantastic idea! It solves housing shortage and makes Den Helder more lively. But because the shortage is high, a developer can easily pay for this without municipal subsidies.

206778.explanation: Holiday parks are meant for holidays, not permanent residence. Therefore, we disagree with these homes as a fixed residential address. However, considering the housing shortage, we want to allow temporary residence. Everyone deserves a roof over their head.

206779.explanation: Raising the income limit costs a lot of money and does not solve the real problem. We believe more in tailor-made solutions: helping people with specific costs. And above all: helping people get work. A job is the best solution against poverty.

206780.explanation: Employers are urgently seeking staff at the moment. There is enough work. We prefer to invest the money in guidance and retraining toward real jobs. That offers people a better future than an artificial job with subsidies.

206781.explanation: Life is already expensive enough. If we stop forgiving debts, people with low incomes will only get more debts. This creates bigger problems and ultimately costs society even more.

206782.explanation: Various trials have already been conducted in the country, including in Den Helder. We believe that work is the best social security. We prefer to invest in jobs and a strong economy.

206783.explanation: Youth work is very effective. It prevents young people from getting into trouble or causing nuisance. Cutting back on this is 'penny wise, pound foolish'.

206784.explanation: Randomly designating parking spaces as charging spots increases parking pressure. Parking spaces are already scarce in some neighborhoods. We only assign a charging spot after research shows there is a real need on that street.

206785.explanation: For people who cannot use public transport, there is the Wmo taxi. Additionally, we must ensure public transport is accessible to everyone. Setting up a separate new system is, in our opinion, expensive and unnecessary.

206786.explanation: In residential areas, 30 km/h is fine for safety. But not throughout the entire built-up area. On major through roads, traffic should be able to proceed normally (50 km/h).

206787.explanation: Good accessibility is crucial for our port and tourism. We will continue to work hard with the province of North Holland and the national government for better connections to the highways.

206788.explanation: Free parking is unique to Den Helder and attracts visitors to our center. We should keep it that way. However, we must look at ways to reduce parking nuisance in the surrounding residential areas.

206789.explanation: New technologies, such as small safe reactors (SMR), suit our city. They help with the transition to clean energy and create jobs.

206790.explanation: We believe it is important to allow choice. But if a heat pump or district heating is more advantageous for the neighborhood, we will definitely encourage it. Affordability is paramount.

206791.explanation: We want to encourage people, not force them. Insulating your home is good for your wallet and the environment, but it must be affordable. We will not burden homeowners with costly obligations.

206792.explanation: More money is not always the solution. We need to look at how we can deploy teams more effectively so they spend less time on rules and more time with their families.

206793.explanation: We are fond of wildlife shelters. We agree, but only on the condition that this fits within the resources we have already allocated for construction. The municipality cannot spend unlimited money.

They must know and respect our norms and values. People without rights are returned immediately to their country of origin.

206803.explanation: We want waste to be separated centrally again, instead of by residents themselves. This is cheaper and works better. Our city currently has some of the highest costs for household waste collection. This must change.

206805.explanation: We welcome an additional professional for safety purposes and to increase the feeling of security. What this person is called is not relevant to us.

206806.explanation: Enforcement of all laws and regulations has the highest priority. But this must be looked at with a principle of customization. Asking if there is a reason why someone does not comply is a question that must be posed and weighed out of compassion.

206807.explanation: In new projects we take into account water management, biodiversity, and more greenery. Think of trees, permeable paving, and protecting the 'Green Lung.' We also create these lungs more and more often.

206808.explanation: We propose restructuring. We want better care for people without a roof over their heads. Homeless people in Den Helder have the right to safe shelter, good guidance, and the right help. Think of support with debts, addiction care, mentorship, or help with independent living.

206809.explanation: Schools must be easily accessible to everyone without costing much time or money. If something is missing financially or materially, we will fill this according to good agreements. The quality and youth must not suffer, and there must be no more distinctions.

206810.explanation: We are fully open to (neighborhood) batteries, hydrogen, or large-scale projects, but residents must benefit from this in their fixed costs.

206811.explanation: A district heating network will only be installed if residents have a financial advantage.

206813.explanation: Personnel issues, administration, and the availability of staff will no longer be reasons for this problem to recur. Furthermore, we believe that service providers/caregivers, as well as informal caregivers, must be taken seriously when applying for their clients.

206814.explanation: This requires only simple follow-up. For example: a leg that has unfortunately been amputated will not grow back by itself.

206815.explanation: We want clean streets and neighborhoods where you feel at home. Residents often know exactly what their neighborhood needs. We want to give them more influence, for example through neighborhood budgets with leadership, so residents can together decide what can be improved in their street or neighborhood.

206816.explanation: A permit to cut down a tree should not be cheaper than a big pot with a plant at a hardware or garden center. Invest in more trees and greenery now and in the coming periods. We aim to achieve a percentage per neighborhood and meet the national 3-30-300 standard.

206818.explanation: In our center, the shortage of parking spaces is worsened by traffic wanting to go to Texel but unwilling to pay the expensive car ticket. Also, more cars per household in neighborhoods contribute. This must be prevented by tackling the problem, not by introducing paid parking.

206820.explanation: We work closely with local businesses so they can hire more people and we know what skills are needed. We are expanding our network.

We want to give them more influence, for example through neighborhood budgets with leadership, so residents can together decide what can be improved in their street or neighborhood.

statements.title: The municipality should urge the national and provincial governments to build the N77 road to make Den Helder more accessible from the highway.
statements.moreInfo: The N77 is a planned provincial road in the Kop van Noord-Holland that runs from the A7 (between Middenmeer and Wieringerwerf) through the Wieringermeerpolder toward Van Ewijcksluis to connect there to the N99. Building the road will make Den Helder more accessible from the A7.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
statements.moreInfo: In Den Helder residents only receive financial assistance if they earn less than 120% of the social assistance norm: the amount the government considers the minimum needed to live. For single adults this amount is about €1,400 in 2026 and for cohabiting people about €2,000 per month. If the municipality wants to help more people, it can raise the income limit (for example to 130% of the social assistance norm).

statements.title: The municipality should prevent construction in the Groene Long nature area.
statements.moreInfo: The Groene Long is a nature area between the Stad binnen de Linie, De Schooten and Nieuw Den Helder neighborhoods. There is debate about the future of the Groene Long as either a nature area or (also) a residential area.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in Den Helder.

The obligation to look for work if you are unemployed and receiving benefits.

statements.title: Den Helder should have city marines.

titleParts.glossary: Special officials tasked with reducing nuisance and crime to increase safety. Unlike a police officer or a municipal enforcement officer, city marshals have no investigative powers.

shootoutStatements.title: The tourist tax should be increased.
When allocating social rental housing, teachers, nurses and police officers should be given priority.
The municipality should cut spending on heritage conservation.
Den Helder should carry out more experiments with a basic income.
The residents of Den Helder should be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.
The municipality must prevent agricultural land in Den Helder from being converted into nature areas.
Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.
The municipality's starter loan should remain available for people buying a house for the first time.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.
The municipality must prohibit new-build homes from receiving a gas connection.

shootoutStatements.theme: Alternative housing options for the elderly
shootoutStatements.title: The municipality must provide care homes, care community houses and other housing options for the elderly, and more knarrenhofjes and support for informal caregivers.
The municipality should reintroduce the dog tax.
The municipality should allow the establishment of new coffeeshops.
The municipality should ask the police to ensure district officers are present in their neighborhoods every day.
The municipality should continue funding a promotion campaign for Den Helder as an attractive location for businesses.
Vacant offices should be converted into housing with subsidies from the municipality.
Residents with a permanent disability should be able to apply to the municipality for lifelong care or assistive devices.
Business parks in Den Helder may be allowed to expand.
Residents who want to create neighborhood or front-yard gardens should receive funding from the municipality.
Residents should be able to take over the management of, for example, a public green space from the municipality.
The use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should be allowed to continue in Den Helder.
Den Helder should continue to provide shelter to asylum seekers who are not allowed to stay in the Netherlands.
Den Helder should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who are available 24 hours a day.
Employers in agriculture and horticulture must provide and arrange housing for seasonal workers on their own company premises.
When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.
The municipality should invest money in improving all school buildings during the next council term.
The municipality should cut spending on youth work.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.
Residents of Den Helder should always be able to decide for themselves whether to make their homes more sustainable.
In the next council term the municipality must build the new wildlife rescue center De Helderse Vallei.
Parking in the city center should remain free.
Den Helder should work to ban bulb cultivation in certain parts of the municipality.
The municipality should more actively invite local businesses to participate in contract tenders.
The municipality should charge residents for their waste by the kilogram.
For every tree cut down in the municipality, a new one must be planted.
